content:
Key Findings

  • Wyre Council spent £326.5M with LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL between 2017/18 and 2025/26
  • The top 5 suppliers accounted for £583.3M or 85.4% of total spending
  • £81.6M was spent with suppliers listed as UNKNOWN
  • BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD received £39.2M over the period
  • The remaining 1,553 suppliers combined received £66.7M or 9.8% of total spending

Wyre Council's Spending Landscape

Wyre Council's spending data reveals a significant concentration of funds with a small number of suppliers. Over the eight-year period from 2017/18 to 2025/26, the council spent a total of £683.0M across 1,563 unique suppliers. The top 10 suppliers alone accounted for £616.3M or 90.2% of this total spend. This concentration raises questions about the diversity of suppliers and potential opportunities for smaller businesses to engage with the council.

The largest single recipient of funds was LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, which received £326.5M or 47.8% of the total spend. This substantial allocation highlights the interdependence between local authorities and the services they provide. The second-largest recipient was the DEPARTMENT FOR COMMUNITIES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT, which received £116.3M or 17.0% of the total spend. This significant expenditure underscores the role of central government funding in supporting local council operations.

Top Suppliers and Value for Money

The top five suppliers to Wyre Council received a combined total of £583.3M, representing 85.4% of the total spend. This concentration of spending suggests that a small number of suppliers play a critical role in delivering essential services. However, it also raises questions about the potential for competition and innovation in the supply chain. The presence of UNKNOWN suppliers, which received £81.6M or 11.9% of the total spend, is particularly noteworthy. This lack of transparency could indicate gaps in the council's procurement processes or challenges in tracking expenditures.

Among the top suppliers, BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD stands out with £39.2M in contracts. This substantial investment in civil engineering services highlights the council's focus on infrastructure and development projects. Other notable suppliers include LANCASHIRE FIRE AUTHORITY with £19.9M and VEOLIA ES (UK) LTD with £16.7M. These figures suggest a significant allocation of resources to public safety and environmental services.

Comparing Key Suppliers

The following table provides a detailed breakdown of the top 10 suppliers to Wyre Council, highlighting the distribution of funds and the percentage of total spend allocated to each supplier.

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £326.5M 47.8%
2 DEPARTMENT FOR COMMUNITIES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT £116.3M 17.0%
3 UNKNOWN £81.6M 11.9%
4 BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD £39.2M 5.7%
5 LANCASHIRE FIRE AUTHORITY £19.9M 2.9%
6 VEOLIA ES (UK) LTD £16.7M 2.4%
7 F PARKINSON LTD £5.7M 0.8%
8 POLICE AND CRIME COMMISSIONER FOR LANCASHIRE £4.3M 0.6%
9 FYLDE COAST YMCA £3.5M 0.5%
10 ASSOCIATED BRITISH PORTS £2.8M 0.4%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about the nature of contracts with UNKNOWN suppliers
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about procurement processes and value for money
  • Check the council's published contracts register to explore the details of high-value contracts
  • Write to your local councillor about the concentration of spending with a small number of suppliers
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends over time
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
